## [00:00 - 03:04]

Creating content on social media can feel intimidating. Think about all the times you have to post, all the platforms, all the formats you can and should use to share your message. But it doesn't have to be complicated. It doesn't have to be overwhelming. Here's the tutorial on how you can leverage Gigi to use one piece of content multiple ways. Hey, everyone. It's Lane with the Mastermind team, and I'm back with a tutorial to show you how easy it is to use Gigi to turn one piece of content into multiple pieces of content for multiple different purposes. So before we get started, we actually need that original piece of content. So for the purposes of this tutorial, I am pretending that I'm a coach who helps women in their 40s reenter the workforce after being home with their children. So I have created a short article or blog post that's called the Top 7 Essential Tips for Women Reintering the Workforce. It's only about 300 words. It's not very deep, but it does give Gigi enough information to work with. So I've got that on hand, so you're going to want to create that and have that on hand as well. Then you're going to log into your Mastermind account, navigate over to the left-hand column, click on Ask Gigi, and then we can either type our message in here, or we can use the Talk to Text option by clicking the microphone, which is what I prefer to do. So before I give her that blog post, though, I'm going to tell her what we're doing. So this is the prompt I'm going to use. Hi, Gigi. I am a coach who helps women in their 40s reenter the workforce after being home with their children. I have written a blog post that I would like to turn into other pieces of content. Can I give you that blog post and then tell you what other pieces of content I would like to generate? So once I click on the microphone again, she transcribes that, and I'm going to submit it with the blue arrow. And she says, absolutely. So now I'm going to go back over to my blog post. I'm going to copy it, and then I'm just going to paste it in. So I'm going to say, here is the blog post. And then I just paste that right in, and then I hit Submit. And then she says, thank you. And then she says, thank you. She can help me repurpose this content. And she gives me suggestions. She says it could be social media posts, infographics, video scripts, email newsletters, or even a mini ebook. That is fantastic. So let's start out by asking her to help us turn that blog post into a script for a real video on Instagram. Can you help me turn that blog post into a real script for Instagram? And if you're not familiar with reels, those are the short form video content on Instagram. So then I hit Submit, and she says, here is my script. And she's got it ready to go. And I can just read this as I'm recording to the video.

## [03:04 - 06:06]

And she even gave me hashtags that I can use. I love that. Now let's see what else she can do with this. Let's ask her to turn that blog post into five social media posts, including suggested images and hashtags. Can you turn that same blog post into five different social media posts, including suggested images and hashtags? Hit the submit with the blue arrow. And she says, here we go. And she says, post number one, you can see a professional woman updating her LinkedIn profile. And it's got the caption with the hashtags. Post two, woman attending a networking event with the caption. Post three, woman working on her personal brand with the hashtags, et cetera, et cetera. She's got them all here for us. And she says, use these as ideas for inspiration on social media. We can change the caption, images, and hashtags to align with our brand voice and audience preferences. Now let's see what else she can do. Let's ask her to make some emails. Can you turn that same blog post into a series of short emails I can send to my email list? Each email should focus on a different tip and should have a different, clear call to action at the end. Please include an enticing subject line. So we're going to ask her to make multiple different emails for us. And look how quickly. She's got the first one here. Email number one with the subject line. And she's got the call to action. Stay tuned for more tips. Email number two, subject line, boost your skills. Email number three, revamp your resume. Email number four. Email number five. Isn't that amazing? So let's not stop there.
